variables.description: Whether the state is described as federal, confederal, or unitary
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Federal; 2=Confederal; 3=Unitary; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether the constitution recognizes any subnational governments. Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): FEDERAL_1=Local/Municipal Government; FEDERAL_2=Subsidiary units (regions, states, provinces, districts, departments); FEDERAL_3=Autonomous Indigenous Groups; FEDERAL_96=Other; FEDERAL_97=Unable to Determine; FEDERAL_98=Not Specified

variables.description: Which level of government has superior legal status in case of conflict between national and subnational law
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Laws of the federal/national government are superior; 2=Laws of subnational government are superior; 3=Depends on topic; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether the constitution allows review of subnational legislation by federal judicial or other central government organs
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether there are provisions for the secession or withdrawal of parts of the state
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes, subsidiary units only; 2=Yes, but unspecified about who may do so; 3=No, secession not allowed; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified

variables.description: Political rights or benefits specifically granted to indigenous groups (asked only if FEDERAL_3=1). Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): INDPOLGR_1=Right to vote; INDPOLGR_2=Right to representation in the central government; INDPOLGR_3=Right to establish political parties; INDPOLGR_4=Right to not pay taxes (for certain activities); INDPOLGR_5=Right to participate in activities illegal for non-indigenous groups; INDPOLGR_6=Right to internal governance; INDPOLGR_7=Cultural rights; INDPOLGR_90=Left to non-constitutional law; INDPOLGR_96=Other; INDPOLGR_97=Unable to Determine; INDPOLGR_98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether indigenous groups are explicitly granted full citizenship (asked only if FEDERAL_3=1)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether the constitution refers to political parties (including reference to specific parties, banning parties, or regulation of parties)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for a right to form political parties (must be explicit, not implied by freedom of association)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether the constitution expresses a preference for one or more political parties
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes, all parties preferred; 2=Yes, certain parties preferred; 3=Yes, certain types of parties preferred; 4=No preference; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether the constitution prohibits one or more political parties
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes, ALL parties prohibited; 2=Yes, certain parties prohibited; 3=Yes, certain types of parties prohibited; 4=No prohibition; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Who is given the power to make determinations of unconstitutional political parties (asked only if PARTPRH=2 or 3). Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): PARTUNCO_1=Executive; PARTUNCO_2=Legislature; PARTUNCO_3=Judicial; PARTUNCO_4=Constitutional Court; PARTUNCO_5=Central Election Commission; PARTUNCO_6=Electoral Court; PARTUNCO_90=Left to non-constitutional law; PARTUNCO_96=Other; PARTUNCO_97=Unable to Determine; PARTUNCO_98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for the ability of individuals to propose legislative initiatives (referenda from below)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for the ability to propose a referendum or plebiscite
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: Who can propose a referendum (asked only if REFEREN=1). Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): REFERENP_1=Head of State; REFERENP_2=Head of Government; REFERENP_3=First/only Chamber; REFERENP_4=Second Chamber; REFERENP_5=Both Chambers required; REFERENP_6=Government/Cabinet; REFERENP_7=Judiciary; REFERENP_8=Ombudsman; REFERENP_9=Triggered automatically by veto of legislation; REFERENP_90=Left to non-constitutional law; REFERENP_96=Other; REFERENP_97=Unable to Determine; REFERENP_98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether the constitution places any restrictions on the right to vote
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 97=Unable to Determine. Note: coded Yes if voting conditions are left to non-constitutional law.

variables.description: Minimum age limit for voting (asked only if VOTERES=1)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=18; 2=20; 3=21; 4=Adult/majority; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Additional restrictions on voting beyond age (asked only if VOTERES=1). Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): VOTELIM_1=Must not be incapacitated; VOTELIM_2=Must not have been convicted of a crime; VOTELIM_3=Must be a man; VOTELIM_4=Must have particular ethnic/national/racial/religious identity; VOTELIM_5=Must be a citizen; VOTELIM_6=Must be literate; VOTELIM_7=Property or monetary restriction; VOTELIM_8=Must speak official language; VOTELIM_9=Must reside in district for certain period; VOTELIM_10=Must not be absent on election day; VOTELIM_11=Must be registered; VOTELIM_12=Must be a national; VOTELIM_13=Must be married; VOTELIM_14=Cannot be in military; VOTELIM_15=Cannot be clergy; VOTELIM_90=Left to non-constitutional law; VOTELIM_96=Other; VOTELIM_97=Unable to Determine; VOTELIM_98=Not Specified
variables.notes: VOTELIM_3 (must be male), VOTELIM_4 (ethnic/racial/religious identity) are directly relevant to equality dimension

variables.description: Whether the constitution makes an explicit claim to universal adult suffrage
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine. Must be explicitly stated. Language like 'voting is universal', 'all citizens can vote', 'adult suffrage' qualifies.

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for an electoral commission or electoral court to oversee the election process
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Electoral Commission; 2=Electoral Court; 3=Both; 4=Neither; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: Whether the constitution makes voting mandatory, at least for some elections (includes explicit compulsion or penalties for non-voting)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: Whether the constitution prescribes that electoral ballots be secret
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: Whether there are provisions for the public financing of election campaigns
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: Whether there are provisions for limits on money used for election campaigns
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine
